name: Save the Cat
description: 15-beat screenplay structure from Blake Snyder's influential screenwriting guide. Designed to create a satisfying audience experience with clear emotional beats.

children.title: Opening Image
children.synopsis: A visual that represents the starting point and tone of the story.

scenes.title: Opening Image
scenes.synopsis: Establish the world and the hero's current state before the journey begins.

children.title: Theme Stated
children.synopsis: Someone states the theme — usually to the hero, who doesn't yet understand it.

scenes.title: Theme Stated
scenes.synopsis: The thematic premise is hinted at or spoken aloud.

children.title: Set-Up
children.synopsis: Introduce the hero's world, flaws, and the things that need fixing.

scenes.title: Set-Up
scenes.synopsis: Show the hero's ordinary world and establish what's missing or broken.

children.title: Catalyst
children.synopsis: Something happens that changes everything — the inciting incident.

scenes.title: Catalyst
scenes.synopsis: A life-changing event disrupts the hero's status quo.

children.title: Debate
children.synopsis: The hero debates whether to accept the challenge or call to adventure.

scenes.title: Debate
scenes.synopsis: Internal or external conflict about whether to take the leap.

children.title: Break into Two
children.synopsis: The hero makes a choice and enters the new world of Act II.

scenes.title: Break into Two
scenes.synopsis: The hero commits to the journey and crosses the threshold.

children.title: B Story
children.synopsis: A secondary story begins — often a love interest or mentor relationship.

scenes.title: B Story
scenes.synopsis: Introduce the relationship that carries the theme.

children.title: Fun and Games
children.synopsis: The promise of the premise — the audience gets what they came for.

scenes.title: Fun and Games
scenes.synopsis: Deliver on the concept. This is the heart of the trailer.

children.title: Midpoint
children.synopsis: A false victory or false defeat that raises the stakes.

scenes.title: Midpoint
scenes.synopsis: The stakes are raised. The fun is over; things get serious.

children.title: Bad Guys Close In
children.synopsis: External pressures mount and internal team dissent grows.

scenes.title: Bad Guys Close In
scenes.synopsis: Opposition tightens its grip. Allies may fracture.

children.title: All Is Lost
children.synopsis: The lowest point — a whiff of death (literal or metaphorical).

scenes.title: All Is Lost
scenes.synopsis: The hero hits rock bottom. Something or someone is lost.

children.title: Dark Night of the Soul
children.synopsis: The hero wallows in hopelessness before finding the strength to go on.

scenes.title: Dark Night of the Soul
scenes.synopsis: Despair and reflection before the final push.

children.title: Break into Three
children.synopsis: The hero discovers the solution, combining A and B stories.

scenes.title: Break into Three
scenes.synopsis: Inspiration strikes. The hero knows what must be done.

children.title: Finale
children.synopsis: The hero confronts the problem using lessons learned throughout the story.

scenes.title: Finale
scenes.synopsis: The climactic sequence where the hero proves transformation.

children.title: Final Image
children.synopsis: The opposite of the opening image — proof that change has occurred.

scenes.title: Final Image
scenes.synopsis: Mirror the opening to show how far the hero has come.
